_header.purpose: Observability stack saturation dashboard showing EC2 CPU, memory, and load metrics from node-exporter
_header.scope: EC2 instance resource utilization for the observability stack using node_exporter metrics
_header.overview: Extracts the Saturation - Observability Stack (EC2) row from the golden signals overview into a dedicated detail dashboard. Displays EC2 CPU utilization, memory utilization from node-exporter, and stat panels for CPU %, Memory %, and load average. Enables focused investigation of observability infrastructure saturation.
_header.dependencies: Mimir datasource (uid: mimir), node_exporter metrics (node_cpu_seconds_total, node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes, node_memory_MemTotal_bytes, node_load1)
_header.interfaces: PromQL queries against node_exporter metric names
_header.related: obs-summary.json for overview, obs-use.json for full USE method analysis
_header.implementation: Grafana dashboard JSON schema version 39 with time series and stat panels for node-exporter metrics

title: Saturation
description: Is the observability EC2 instance running out of resources? The time series panels show CPU and memory utilization trends — look for sustained climbs toward the red zone. The stat panels below give instant readings. If CPU is saturated, Grafana queries will time out and metric ingestion falls behind. If memory is saturated, containers risk OOM kills. Use the load average to distinguish CPU-bound (high load + high CPU) from I/O-bound (high load + low CPU) saturation. For per-container breakdown, check the Obs Summary dashboard.

panels.title: EC2 CPU Utilization
panels.description: CPU utilization trend for the observability EC2 instance. Green (<70%) is healthy. A sustained climb into yellow indicates growing load — check if metric ingestion volume has increased. Red (>90%) means CPU-bound: dashboard queries will be slow and metric scraping may miss intervals. The smooth line interpolation makes trends easier to spot than raw samples.

panels.title: EC2 Memory Utilization
panels.description: Memory utilization trend for the observability EC2 instance. A gradual upward slope over hours or days indicates a memory leak in one of the observability containers (Mimir and Tempo are common offenders). Red (>90%) means OOM kill risk — the kernel will terminate the container using the most memory, causing data loss and service restart.

panels.title: CPU %
panels.description: Current CPU utilization as a single number. Quick glance indicator — green means healthy, yellow means under pressure, red means saturated and impacting performance.

panels.title: Memory %
panels.description: Current memory utilization as a single number. If this is red, check the Obs Summary dashboard to identify which container is consuming the most memory and consider restarting it or adjusting its memory limits.

panels.title: Load Average (1m)
panels.description: 1-minute load average — processes waiting for CPU. Compare against CPU core count. Load exceeding core count means work is queuing. High load with low CPU% points to I/O wait (disk is the bottleneck, common when Mimir or Tempo compacts data).
